title: rebuild writes Entry with 'id' prop not 'entry_id', breaks recall
description:
After 'cortex rebuild' completes successfully (292 datoms, 17 entries), 'cortex recall' returns 0 results for queries that worked before rebuild.

Root cause: the rebuild path writes Entry nodes to Neo4j using property 'id' instead of 'entry_id', and stores body as a JSON-quoted string ("\"Retries must...\"" instead of "Retries must..."). Cypher from internal/neo4j queries by e.entry_id, so none of the rebuilt entries are found.

Also: rebuild appears to re-apply on top of existing state (418 Entry nodes for 17 logical entries), not into a clean backend as docs claim.

Reproduction:
1. cortex observe 'Retries must use exponential backoff with full jitter' --kind=Observation --facets=domain:Reliability,project:pay-gw
2. cortex recall 'retry strategy'  # returns results
3. cortex rebuild
4. cortex recall 'retry strategy'  # returns 0

Verification cypher:
  MATCH (e:Entry) WHERE e.entry_id IS NULL RETURN keys(e) LIMIT 1
  # returns keys including 'id' and 'body' (JSON-quoted) — no entry_id
close_reason: Closed

title: GDS projection 'cortex.semantic' never initialized; recall PPR_FAILED on fresh Neo4j
description:
internal/recall uses a named GDS projection 'cortex.semantic' for gds.pageRank.stream calls. Comments across the codebase (internal/neo4j/gds.go:23, internal/community/detect.go:59) claim the projection is 'already created in a prior step via gds.graph.project', but nothing in the codebase actually executes gds.graph.project for it. Verified via grep: no production call to gds.graph.project exists. On a fresh Neo4j container started by cortex up, PPR fails with PPR_FAILED on the first recall invocation because the projection doesn't exist.

Fixed on 2026-04-10 by adding ensureProjection to the neo4jPPRRunner in cmd/cortex/recall_adapters.go. The helper calls gds.graph.exists on the first PPR call of each Neo4j session, and lazily creates a wildcard projection (all node labels, all relationships) if absent. Projections persist in Neo4j's in-memory graph store until restart, so the lazy-create cost is paid at most once per Neo4j session.

Phase 1 follow-up: the wildcard projection is overbroad. Ideal behavior is a scheduled re-projection on cortex up that includes only the node labels and relationship types recall actually traverses. This would also fix the subtle issue that wildcard projections pick up :Concept nodes as 'entries' during PPR result filtering (currently the recall_adapters Cypher filters with n.entry_id STARTS WITH 'entry:' to exclude concept nodes, which is a correct but fragile guard).

Also: the PPR result filter was changed to exclude concept-prefixed IDs because wildcard projection surfaces them during pagerank propagation. Tuning the projection at cortex up time would remove the need for the prefix filter.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ensureProjection runs on the first recall of each Neo4j session. 2. Projection is idempotent; subsequent recalls skip the create. 3. PPR returns only entry-prefixed IDs in its result set. 4. TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip passes on a fresh Neo4j container without any manual gds.graph.project setup.
notes: The 'cortex.semantic' name is referenced in ~5 files. Moving the projection bootstrap to cortex up would be architecturally correct; the lazy-create in recall is the minimal fix to unblock the observe→recall round trip.
close_reason: Fixed via commits dd58c1a + 5bf774d. TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip passes end-to-end against live backends.

title: Recall pipeline depends on :Concept graph but nothing writes :Concept nodes
description:
internal/recall/pipeline.go Stage 2 seed resolution is the ONLY entry point into the PPR→rerank path — there is no standalone cosine search, no BM25 fallback, no degradation mode. The seed resolver at cmd/cortex/recall_adapters.go neo4jSeedResolver.Resolve executes MATCH (c:Concept) and traverses MENTIONS edges to reach :Entry nodes. With zero :Concept nodes in the graph, seeds are empty, PPR runs on an empty seed set, zero entries are loaded, and recall returns results=0 regardless of how well the query matches the stored vectors.

Grep across the entire codebase confirms: NO production code path writes :Concept nodes. The only mentions of :Concept are in read-side code (recall_adapters.go:136) and spec references. The write pipeline never extracts concepts from observation bodies.

This was invisible until now because:
1. internal/recall/pipeline_test.go uses a fake SeedResolver that returns synthetic seed lists, bypassing the Cypher entirely.
2. The round-3 grill review added internal/e2e/observe_recall_roundtrip_test.go but the test never ran against a live Neo4j with an empty graph until 2026-04-10.
3. Comments in the pipeline claim empty concepts 'degenerate to a random-walk style PPR kick from any available seed' but the resolver returns nil on empty input and nothing else fills the seed set.

Fixed on 2026-04-10 via a lexical concept extraction path in the write pipeline (internal/write/concepts.go). The tokenizer splits observation bodies on whitespace + hyphens + punctuation, lowercases, filters stopwords, and the write pipeline's new deriveAndAppendConcepts method builds mentions_concept edge datoms that flow through the existing Neo4j applier. The applier's labelForEntity gained a 'concept' prefix and edgeAttributes gained 'mentions_concept' → 'MENTIONS'. On the read side, neo4jSeedResolver now pipes incoming concepts through write.ExtractConceptTokens so LLM-produced phrases are broken into the same lexical tokens that the write side laid down.

Hyphens are treated as boundaries in both directions because the Ollama concept_extraction prompt returns the three-part split ['cortex', 'roundtrip', 'token'] for a query like 'cortex-roundtrip-token'; write-side hyphen preservation would have broken matching. Underscores survive as connectors.

Resolved by the same commit that closes this bead; see follow-up cortex-proj for the GDS projection issue uncovered by the same e2e run.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Observe path writes :Concept + :MENTIONS to Neo4j via lexical tokenization. 2. Recall pipeline finds seeds via c.entry_id IN $conceptIds. 3. Write-side and read-side tokenizers agree (TestConceptSymmetry). 4. TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip passes.
notes: Design decision: lexical tokenization, not LLM, at write time. Rationale: LLM concept extraction would add 3-10s to every observe. Lexical is O(body length) and deterministic. Future work could add LLM-based concept augmentation as a separate, async pass. The spec comment at internal/recall/pipeline.go:108 about 'random-walk degeneration' is aspirational — there's no actual code path that delivers it. That should be followed up too.
close_reason: Fixed via commits dd58c1a + 5bf774d. TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip passes end-to-end against live backends.

title: weaviate.HTTPClient.Upsert update fallback uses PUT (full replace) and wipes vectors on self-heal replay
description:
After the POST-first fix in bead cortex-b8a, Upsert still fell back to PUT /v1/objects/{class}/{id} for the update path. Weaviate PUT is a FULL REPLACE: any field not present in the request body (including the vector) is wiped on the server. This is incompatible with cortex's applier scratch-bag design, which intends merge semantics.

Consequence: the vector is stripped from every Weaviate Entry whose link-derivation datoms are later replayed by self-heal. The first observe commits the body datom through ApplyWithVector — vector stored OK. Link derivation then appends link datoms to the log (via internal/write/pipeline.go:455 p.Log.Append) but does NOT reapply them to backends. On the next cortex command, self-heal creates a FRESH BackendApplier with an empty vector cache, replays the link datoms as tiny scratch bags (just cortex_id + link_type), and calls Upsert with a nil vector. The POST fails with 422 (already exists), the PUT fallback replaces the entire object with the tiny bag — wiping the embedding. Subsequent cosine search over Entry finds zero hits by semantic similarity, and seed resolution fails too because no :Concept nodes were created (separate issue). Recall returns results=0.

Verified live against Weaviate 1.36.9 on 2026-04-10:
  POST /v1/objects {vector:v1, cortex_id:first} → 200, vector stored
  PUT  /v1/objects/Entry/<id> {no vector, properties:{extra:added}} → 200
  GET  /v1/objects/Entry/<id>?include=vector → vector=null (WIPED)

  PATCH /v1/objects/Entry/<id> {no vector, properties:{link:x}} → 204
  GET  /v1/objects/Entry/<id>?include=vector → vector preserved (768 dim kept)

Fix: change the second attempt in Upsert from PUT to PATCH. PATCH has Weaviate merge semantics — only fields present in the request body are modified, everything else (including the vector) is preserved. This matches cortex's scratch-bag intent: each datom's incremental bag merges into the object without destroying previously-set state.

Why: blocks end-to-end observe→recall under any code path that triggers link derivation (which is every observe with LinkTopK>0 and >=1 neighbor), because the next command's self-heal wipes the vector. This is the actual root cause behind the round-3 CRIT-009 symptom (Weaviate cosine rerank inert) — the CRIT-009 fix fixed the vector-threading in the live-write path, but left the self-heal replay path lethal.

How to apply:
1. internal/weaviate/client.go Upsert: change the update-phase http.MethodPut to http.MethodPatch. Update log messages and the PUT URL label.
2. internal/weaviate/client_test.go fakeWeaviate: serve PATCH on /v1/objects/{class}/{id} instead of PUT. Return 204 No Content (what real Weaviate returns).
3. Verify: run TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip — recall must find the entry by cosine similarity after link derivation has replayed.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Upsert fallback uses PATCH not PUT. 2. Unit tests green. 3. Self-heal replay of a link datom on a previously-vectored entry preserves the vector (verified live: POST with vector, PATCH without vector, GET include=vector returns original vector). 4. TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip recall step returns at least one hit matching the observed token.
notes: Discovered while debugging why e2e recall returned results=0 after all infrastructure bugs (cortex-3z1, cortex-54l, cortex-c09, cortex-b8a) were fixed. The applier's bag design (internal/weaviate/applier.go) is logically correct for a single-invocation write, but the bag is in-memory per invocation — it's lost across process boundaries. Self-heal replay cannot reconstruct the vector, so any update from self-heal MUST be merge-based, not replace-based.
close_reason: Closed

title: weaviate.HTTPClient.Upsert uses PUT-only — fails on first datom because Weaviate PUT requires existing object
description:
internal/weaviate/client.go:258 Upsert issues PUT /v1/objects/{class}/{id} against Weaviate. Weaviate's PUT is update-only — it returns HTTP 500 with body {"error":[{"message":"no object with id '<uuid>'"}]} when the target id does not exist. The cortex applier then calls Upsert for every datom in a transaction group (body, kind, facets, embedding model datoms, etc), and the very first call for a brand-new entity fails because the object has never been created. All 8 datoms for a fresh observe fail with the same 'no object with id' error, surfacing as BACKEND_APPLY_PARTIAL.

Verified live against Weaviate 1.36.9 on 2026-04-10 via direct HTTP probes:
- PUT /v1/objects/Entry/<new-uuid> → 500 'no object with id'
- POST /v1/objects {id=<new-uuid>} → 200 create
- POST /v1/objects {id=<existing-uuid>} → 422 'id ... already exists'
- PUT /v1/objects/Entry/<existing-uuid> → 200 update

Fix: Upsert should try POST /v1/objects first. On HTTP 422 (already exists), fall back to PUT /v1/objects/{class}/{id} for the update case. This matches Weaviate's idiomatic create-or-update pattern and requires no change to the Upsert signature or the BackendApplier.

The older comment at line 271 ('Weaviate returns 200 on update, 201 (some versions) or 204 on create') is incorrect — PUT has never created objects in any Weaviate version; the create path has always been POST. This bug likely shipped because internal/weaviate/client_test.go uses a fake Weaviate that accepts PUT for any id, masking the semantic mismatch.

Why: blocks observe end-to-end; every fresh entity fails to persist to Weaviate, every recall misses cosine-ranked hits. This is a CRIT-009 class failure (Weaviate apply inert) that the round-3 review caught a different symptom of (nil vector) but missed this one.
How to apply:
1. internal/weaviate/client.go Upsert: rewrite to (a) POST /v1/objects with body containing class+id+properties+vector, (b) on 200/201/204 return nil, (c) on 422 with 'already exists' body, re-issue PUT /v1/objects/{class}/{id} with the same body, (d) any other status → error.
2. internal/weaviate/client_test.go: update the fakeWeaviate handler at /v1/objects to distinguish POST-create from PUT-update and track both. Add a test asserting POST is attempted first, then PUT on already-exists conflict.
3. Verify via re-running TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip under -tags='cli integration'.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Upsert POSTs first and falls back to PUT on 422 already-exists. 2. Unit test covers both paths. 3. Integration test TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip observe stage no longer returns BACKEND_APPLY_PARTIAL for weaviate apply failures.
notes: Related: cortex-3z1 (phantom llama3.1 tag), cortex-54l (NEO4J_PASSWORD env), cortex-c09 (digest on /api/tags). Four systemic bridge-adapter bugs uncovered by the first-ever integration run of internal/e2e/observe_recall_roundtrip_test.go on 2026-04-10.
close_reason: Closed

title: ollama.HTTPClient.doShow reads digest from /api/show which returns no digest field — EMBEDDING_MODEL_UNAVAILABLE on every write
description:
internal/ollama/client.go:223 doShow POSTs to /api/show and decodes Digest from the response body, but the real Ollama /api/show endpoint does NOT return a top-level digest field. Verified against Ollama 0.20.5 on 2026-04-10: /api/show keys are license, modelfile, parameters, template, details, model_info, tensors, capabilities, modified_at — no digest. The digest is instead published by /api/tags which cortex already hits from Ping().

Consequence: the write pipeline's FR-051 digest-pinning validation (internal/write — callers of observeEmbedder.ModelDigest) sees an empty digest and fails with EMBEDDING_MODEL_UNAVAILABLE: embedding model returned empty digest. This happens on the very first cortex observe call on any real machine.

Why unit tests miss it: internal/ollama/client_test.go uses a fakeOllama stub that happily returns any JSON shape the test authors chose (including a top-level digest) on the /api/show path. The real endpoint shape is never exercised until an integration test against a live Ollama.

Fix: modify doShow to fetch the digest from GET /api/tags (the same endpoint Ping uses). Walk the returned models array, find the one whose name matches c.embeddingModel (or embeddingModel + ":latest" since the tags response uses canonical tags), return its digest. Continue to use the name fallback the current code already has.

Why: blocks every observe command on any real Ollama >= 0.20 — and probably earlier versions too. This was only missed because the internal/e2e integration test was never run against a live stack before today.
How to apply:
1. internal/ollama/client.go: replace the /api/show POST with a GET /api/tags call; parse {models: [{name, digest}]}; match by embeddingModel with ":latest" fallback; return ModelInfo{Name: matchedName, Digest: matchedDigest}. Keep sync.Once caching and ShowCallCount semantics.
2. Update showResponse struct or replace with tagsResponse (already partially present — see line 60 note about /api/tags).
3. internal/ollama/client_test.go: update fakeOllama to serve /api/tags with a digest-bearing payload when the test wants to verify digest pinning. Update/add a test that asserts ModelDigest(ctx) returns non-empty digest for a model served via /api/tags.
4. Confirm via re-running the e2e observe→recall integration test.
acceptance_criteria: 1. doShow (or its replacement) reads digest from /api/tags, not /api/show. 2. Existing sync.Once / ShowCallCount / caching semantics preserved. 3. Unit test verifies correct digest is returned when /api/tags lists the model. 4. Unit test verifies empty-tag-list case still surfaces a useful error. 5. Integration test TestCLI_ObserveRecallRoundtrip can reach at least Stage 1 of observe without EMBEDDING_MODEL_UNAVAILABLE.
notes: Verified live with: curl http://localhost:11434/api/show -d '{"name":"nomic-embed-text"}' returns no digest key. curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags returns models[] with 20-char digests for both nomic-embed-text:latest and qwen3:4b-instruct. Related: cortex-54l (NEO4J_PASSWORD env wiring), cortex-3z1 (phantom llama3.1:8b-instruct tag). All three are Phase 1 integration-layer bugs that the grill-code review missed because no integration run was ever performed.
close_reason: Closed

title: cortex up never exports NEO4J_PASSWORD to docker compose subprocess — fresh-machine install always auth-fails
description:
internal/infra/docker.go:61 ExecDocker.ComposeUp runs docker compose up -d via exec.CommandContext without explicitly setting cmd.Env. The subprocess inherits the parent process environment. Meanwhile internal/infra/up.go step 2 calls EnsureNeo4jPassword which only writes neo4j_password to ~/.cortex/config.yaml — it never calls os.Setenv or threads the password into the ComposeUp call.

Consequence on a fresh machine:
1. cortex up generates a random password, writes to config.yaml (0600)
2. ComposeUp shells out to docker compose up; NEO4J_PASSWORD is unset in the parent env
3. docker compose expands the compose file's ${NEO4J_PASSWORD:-cortexbootstrap} to the placeholder literal
4. Neo4j container initializes with password=cortexbootstrap and burns it into /data (volume cortex_neo4j_data)
5. cortex's readiness probe opens Bolt with the generated password from config.yaml → 401
6. Every subsequent probe fails; STARTUP_BUDGET_EXCEEDED after ~90s
7. The bootstrap password is now permanently baked into the Neo4j volume — users must docker volume rm cortex_neo4j_data to recover

This is a P0 because it makes US-10 (Start managed infrastructure) impossible on any fresh machine without a manual env override. Directly verified: running NEO4J_PASSWORD=$(grep neo4j_password ~/.cortex/config.yaml | awk '{print $2}') /tmp/cortex up succeeds cleanly.

Fix options:
A. Extend Docker interface: ComposeUp(ctx, composeFile, env map[string]string). up.go reads the password from config right after EnsureNeo4jPassword and passes env={"NEO4J_PASSWORD": password}. ExecDocker.ComposeUp sets cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "NEO4J_PASSWORD=..."). Cleanest.
B. os.Setenv("NEO4J_PASSWORD", password) in up.go before ComposeUp. Quick but pollutes the cortex process env and is racy if cortex ever runs ComposeUp concurrently.
C. Use a compose env-file written to ~/.cortex/compose.env (mode 0600). Matches the comment in docker-compose.yaml line 90 ("env-file injection") and is the path the author apparently intended, but doesn't match what the code does today.

Recommended: option A — a clean interface extension with an explicit env map, plus a unit test asserting cmd.Env contains NEO4J_PASSWORD after ComposeUp is invoked with the map. Fakes in up_test.go also need updating.

Why: blocks cortex up on every fresh machine install. Detected during pre-e2e-run verification on 2026-04-10 when the observe→recall integration test required a live stack.
How to apply:
1. internal/infra/docker.go: extend Docker interface signature for ComposeUp to take env map[string]string; update ExecDocker.ComposeUp to set cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "K=V"...).
2. internal/infra/up.go step 2: capture the password returned by EnsureNeo4jPassword and pass env={"NEO4J_PASSWORD": password} to ComposeUp.
3. internal/infra/up_test.go + down_test.go: update fakeDocker.ComposeUp signature; add a test case verifying the env map is non-empty when up.go invokes it.
4. Confirm on a fresh machine: docker volume rm cortex_neo4j_data; cortex up; expect 'managed stack is ready' without any manual NEO4J_PASSWORD override.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Docker interface ComposeUp signature accepts an env map. 2. up.go threads the generated Neo4j password into the env map before calling ComposeUp. 3. Unit test asserts cmd.Env received by the subprocess contains NEO4J_PASSWORD=<value>. 4. On a fresh machine (docker volume rm cortex_neo4j_data && rm ~/.cortex/config.yaml), cortex up succeeds without any env override and the stack is ready within the startup budget. 5. Downstream cortex observe succeeds against the resulting Neo4j using the config.yaml password.
notes: Implemented: ComposeUp interface now takes env map; Run threads NEO4J_PASSWORD through; test TestRunThreadsNeo4jPasswordIntoComposeEnv asserts config.yaml value reaches subprocess env.
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace phantom llama3.1:8b-instruct tag with qwen3:4b-instruct-2507
description:
cmd/cortex/up.go:36 pins defaultGenerationModel = "llama3.1:8b-instruct", but that exact tag does NOT exist on the Ollama registry. Canonical instruct tags are llama3.1:8b (alias for 8b-instruct-q4_K_M) or the explicit llama3.1:8b-instruct-q4_K_M. As a result, a fresh cortex up on a machine without a pre-pulled model will fail the Ollama readiness probe at infra/up.go:264 (containsModel check) with a missing-model error, blocking US-10 (Start managed infrastructure) entirely.

Fix: switch defaultGenerationModel to qwen3:4b-instruct-2507 (2.5 GB disk, 256K context, non-thinking instruct, released July 2025). Rationale vs llama3.1:8b:
- 2x faster inference (~40-80 tok/s vs 20-40 on M-series)
- Half the RAM footprint (~3-4 GB vs 6-8 GB)
- Half the disk (~2.5 GB vs 4.9 GB)
- Explicit non-thinking variant → no <think> tag risk for ollamaLinkProposer JSON parser
- 256K context window (2x llama3.1:8b, 6x qwen3:8b)
- Newer release (9 mo vs 1 year)

The generation model is used at 6 call sites: observe link derivation, recall concept extraction, ingest synthesis, analyze adapters (2x), trail summary. All are structured-output or moderate-reasoning tasks well within 4B capability.

Why: blocks fresh-machine cortex up; discovered during pre-e2e-run verification.
How to apply:
1. cmd/cortex/up.go:36 — change constant value
2. internal/ollama/client.go:98 — update comment example
3. internal/ollama/client_test.go:92 — update test fixture
4. internal/infra/up_test.go:111 — update test fixture
5. Rebuild + verify go test ./cmd/cortex/... ./internal/ollama/... ./internal/infra/...
acceptance_criteria: 1. defaultGenerationModel resolves to a tag that exists on Ollama registry. 2. cortex up succeeds on a fresh machine with no pre-pulled models. 3. Unit tests across cmd/cortex, internal/ollama, internal/infra continue to pass. 4. Observe→recall roundtrip integration test (internal/e2e) succeeds end-to-end against the pulled model.
close_reason: Fixed defaultGenerationModel constant + 5 test fixture updates. Build green, tests pass. Commit 686843a.

title: Write end-to-end cross-project value fixture test
description:
A test_e2e_cross_project_value integration test ingests two fixture projects sharing a concrete pattern, runs cortex recall for a cross-cutting concept, and asserts the top 10 results include entries from both projects and that cortex analyze --find-patterns produces at least one cross_project=true frame whose exemplars span both projects.

## Inputs
- **fixture_projects** (`list<path>`): two repos sharing a retry-pattern -- Source: test fixtures

## Outputs
- **test_assertions** (`bool`): all pass -- Dest: go test output

## Constraints
- CI-gated per SC-003 and SC-004
acceptance_criteria:
- After ingesting both fixtures, cortex recall 'retry with exponential backoff' returns at least one result from each project in the top 10
- cortex analyze --find-patterns writes at least one frame with cross_project=true
- That frame's DERIVED_FROM edges reference entries in both projects
- The test runs offline (no network access required)
close_reason: Cross-project value e2e fixture landed in internal/e2e/cross_project_value_test.go; passes offline via hermetic fakes.

title: Implement cortex analyze --find-patterns for cross-project frames
description:
cortex analyze --find-patterns accepts clusters with exemplars from at least 2 distinct projects and no more than 70 percent from any one project, applies MDL ratio 1.15, marks accepted frames cross_project=true, boosts importance by +0.20, and performs a full community refresh.

## Inputs
- **min_projects** (`int`): default 2 -- Source: config
- **max_share_per_project** (`f64`): default 0.70 -- Source: config
- **analysis_mdl_ratio** (`f64`): default 1.15 -- Source: config

## Outputs
- **cross_project_frames** (`list<Frame>`): cross_project=true, DERIVED_FROM spans >=2 projects -- Dest: log

## Constraints
- Triggers full community refresh after writes
acceptance_criteria:
- A cluster with exemplars from one project is rejected with reason SINGLE_PROJECT
- A cluster with 8 of 10 exemplars from project A is rejected with reason PROJECT_SHARE_EXCEEDED
- Accepted cross-project frames have cross_project=true and their DERIVED_FROM edges reference entries in at least 2 distinct projects
- Migrated (migrated=true) entries are excluded by default and included only with --include-migrated
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement cortex ingest command with post-ingest reflect
description:
cortex ingest <path> --project=<name> walks the repo, groups modules by strategy, summarizes each module via Ollama with ingest.ollama_concurrency workers, writes entries through the observe pipeline, synthesizes an ingest trail, and runs scoped reflection when post_ingest_reflect=true.

## Inputs
- **project_path** (`string`): absolute path -- Source: CLI argument
- **project_name** (`string`): required -- Source: --project flag
- **ollama_concurrency** (`int`): default 4 -- Source: config

## Outputs
- **ingest_trail_id** (`string`): trail:ingest:<project>:<timestamp> -- Dest: log
- **module_entry_ids** (`list<string>`): one per ingested module -- Dest: log

## Constraints
- post_ingest_analyze defaults to false
- Honor timeouts.ingest_summary_seconds per module
acceptance_criteria:
- Ingesting a fixture Go project with three packages produces three module entries and one synthesized ingest trail
- Running ingest twice against an unchanged project writes zero new entries on the second run (or reports no-op)
- cortex ingest status --project=<name> reports the last ingested commit SHA, timestamp, and counts
- cortex ingest resume --project=<name> processes only missing modules and does not duplicate already-written entries
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement cortex reflect with per-frame watermark
description:
cortex reflect selects candidate episodic clusters since the per-frame reflection watermark, evaluates 3-exemplar/2-timestamp/0.65-cosine/1.3-MDL thresholds, proposes frames via LLM, writes accepted frames with DERIVED_FROM provenance, and advances the watermark per accepted frame.

## Inputs
- **reflection_watermark** (`string(ulid)`): last accepted frame tx -- Source: neo4j metadata
- **min_cluster_size** (`int`): default 3 -- Source: config
- **avg_pairwise_cosine_floor** (`f64`): default 0.65 -- Source: config
- **mdl_ratio** (`f64`): default 1.3 -- Source: config

## Outputs
- **frame_datoms** (`list<Datom>`): typed semantic/procedural frames -- Dest: log

## Constraints
- Scoped markers (post-ingest, --contradictions-only, --project=) are separate from the global watermark
acceptance_criteria:
- A cluster of 2 exemplars is rejected and logged with reason BELOW_MIN_CLUSTER_SIZE
- A qualifying cluster produces a frame with DERIVED_FROM edges to every exemplar
- An interrupted run that wrote 3 of 5 proposed frames resumes on the next invocation without reprocessing the 3 already-written frames
- cortex reflect --dry-run --explain prints candidate clusters and rejection reasons and writes zero datoms
close_reason: internal/reflect package with per-frame watermark advancement; 10 tests cover all 4 AC and every rejection reason.

title: Implement default recall pipeline with HippoRAG and ACT-R
description:
cortex recall in default mode extracts concepts, resolves seed nodes, runs Personalized PageRank, reranks by ACT-R activation using weights 0.3/0.3/0.3/0.1, returns the top 10 results with trail and community context and 'why surfaced' traces, and writes reinforcement datoms for each result.

## Inputs
- **query** (`string`): non-empty -- Source: CLI argument
- **seed_top_k** (`int`): default 5 -- Source: config
- **ppr_damping** (`f64`): default 0.85 -- Source: config
- **ppr_max_iterations** (`int`): default 20 -- Source: config
- **visibility_threshold** (`f64`): default 0.05 inclusive -- Source: config

## Outputs
- **results** (`list<RecallResult>`): length <= retrieval.default_limit -- Dest: stdout
- **reinforcement_datoms** (`list<Datom>`): base_activation, retrieval_count, last_retrieved_at per result -- Dest: log

## Constraints

title: Implement cortex observe write pipeline
description:
cortex observe validates payload, scans for secrets, resolves PSI subject, computes embedding, appends a transaction-group to the log, then outside the lock applies datoms to Neo4j and Weaviate, derives links, updates watermarks, and prints the new entry ID.

## Outputs

- **entry_datoms** (`list<Datom>`): one tx group -- Dest: log

- Backend apply failures do not roll back the committed log

title: Implement self-healing backend replay on command startup
description:
Before any read-dependent command proceeds, the system compares log max(tx) against Neo4j and Weaviate watermarks and replays every missing committed datom into the lagging backend, then updates the watermark.

## Inputs
- **log_reader** (`iterator<Datom>`): merge-sorted -- Source: log-merge-sort-reader
- **backend_watermarks** (`Watermark`): current values -- Source: backend-watermarks

## Outputs
- **applied_datoms** (`int`): per backend, strictly non-negative -- Dest: return value
- **updated_watermarks** (`Watermark`): equals log max(tx) on success -- Dest: backend-watermarks

## Constraints
- LWW attributes collapse to the highest-tx value during replay
- Non-LWW datoms are replayed in order
acceptance_criteria:
- Given log max(tx)=T5 and neo4j watermark=T3, exactly the T4 and T5 datoms are applied to Neo4j
- Given matching watermarks on both backends, zero datoms are applied and the command proceeds immediately
- Commands recall, reflect, analyze, subject merge, communities, community show, path, trail show, trail list, history, and as-of all invoke the replay path on startup

title: Implement multi-segment merge-sort reader by tx ULID

title: Implement torn-tail validation and truncation on startup
description:
On startup the log package validates the last log.tail_validation_window_bytes of every segment, truncates any torn segment back to its last well-formed datom, and appends a log.recovered audit datom describing the truncation.

## Inputs

- **segment_files** (`list<file>`): all *.jsonl under ~/.cortex/log.d -- Source: directory scan

- **log_recovered_datom** (`Datom`): one per truncation -- Dest: current active segment

## Constraints
- Must not rewrite bytes earlier than the torn boundary
- Must not delete the segment file even if the entire tail is unreadable
acceptance_criteria:
- A segment ending in a half-written line is truncated to the byte offset of the last valid checksummed datom
- A segment with an intact tail is not modified (mtime unchanged)

title: Implement per-segment append writer with flock and fsync

The log package opens a segment file under ~/.cortex/log.d/<ulid>-<writer>.jsonl with O_APPEND, acquires an exclusive flock with a 5 second timeout, writes a transaction-group of datoms, fsyncs once, releases the lock, and returns the committed tx.

title: analyze/reflect never produce candidates: community nodes never written
description:
## Summary

Both `cortex analyze --find-patterns` and `cortex reflect` return 0 candidates on a populated system (~600 content entries, 2,464 retrieval reinforcements across 48,334 datoms in ~/.cortex/log.d) because they query Neo4j for `(:Community {level: 0})<-[:IN_COMMUNITY]-(e)` nodes that don't exist.

## Root cause — chicken-and-egg

1. The write pipeline (internal/write, internal/neo4j/applier.go) never creates `:Community` nodes or `:IN_COMMUNITY` edges. Greps across internal/write and internal/neo4j show zero writers.
2. The only path that writes community nodes is `community.Detector.Persist()` at internal/community/detect.go:250 (`MERGE (n)-[:IN_COMMUNITY {level: \$level}]->(c)`).
3. That path is reachable only from `communityRefresherBridge.Refresh()` in cmd/cortex/analyze_adapters.go:237.
4. The refresh bridge runs only AFTER `analyze.Pipeline` has accepted and written candidates.
5. But candidate enumeration (analyze_adapters.go:52 and reflect_adapters.go:55) queries the same `:Community` nodes that don't exist yet.

Net: zero candidates → no writes → no refresh → no communities → zero candidates forever.

The reflect_adapters.go:47-52 comment acknowledges the gap:

    // Until the write-side applier populates those properties this returns empty;
    // the threshold filter in the reflect pipeline drops each candidate as
    // BELOW_COSINE_FLOOR or similar, which is the correct 'no work to do' behaviour.

The recall pipeline (recall_adapters.go:280-308) does build a GDS projection, but only ephemerally — it drops it after PPR, so Leiden never has a persistent projection to run against.

## Evidence

- \`cortex communities\` → \`(no communities at this level)\`
- \`cortex analyze --find-patterns --dry-run --explain\` → \`[dry-run] 0 candidates evaluated, 0 accepted\`
- \`cortex reflect --dry-run --explain\` → \`[dry-run] 0 candidates evaluated, 0 accepted, watermark=\`
- On a system with 5,642 log segments, ~48,334 datoms, 600+ content entries.

## Fix options

1. **Bootstrap path (recommended):** add a \`cortex communities detect\` (or similar) subcommand that creates a persistent semantic GDS projection, runs \`community.Detector.Detect\` with Leiden/Louvain fallback, and persists the hierarchy. Invoke it opportunistically after ingest / observe batches. Matches the existing code comment in analyze.go that says 'the caller is responsible for having created the GDS projection via cortex up / rebuild' — today nothing actually does.
2. **Applier path:** have internal/neo4j/applier.go maintain a cheap level-0 community assignment (by project or by semantic-similarity bucket) as datoms land. Heavier change but eliminates the explicit detect step.
3. **Analyze self-bootstrap:** if \`Candidates\` returns 0 on first pass, have the bridge run a one-shot detect+persist before giving up. Minimal diff but hides the dependency.

Option 1 is the smallest correct change.

## Acceptance

- On a populated system (≥10 entries, ≥2 projects), \`cortex analyze --find-patterns --dry-run\` returns at least one candidate.
- \`cortex communities\` lists at least one level-0 community.
- Zero-entry system still exits 0 gracefully with no results.
notes: Fix implemented locally (pending commit). Approach: bootstrap path (Option 1 from the description). Added cortex communities detect subcommand in cmd/cortex/communities.go that calls a new ensureSemanticProjection helper (extracted from neo4jPPRRunner.refreshProjection into cmd/cortex/projection.go), then community.Detector.Detect with Leiden+Louvain fallback, then Detector.Persist. Detection-only — no LLM summarizer pass — keeps the bootstrap Ollama-independent. Verified live: cortex communities went from (no communities) to 83 level-0 communities; cortex analyze --find-patterns --dry-run went from 0 candidates evaluated to 83. The 0 acceptances are now correct (all rejected SINGLE_PROJECT — only one project ingested locally). Whole-repo go test ./... passes with zero regressions. Three unit tests added in cmd/cortex/communities_test.go (happy-path Leiden, Louvain fallback, both-fail). Note: cortex reflect is still 0 candidates but for a DIFFERENT reason — separate follow-up bead being filed.

title: ingest: size-gate per-package modules with per-file fallback
description: cmd/cortex and other large Go packages hit num_ctx*prompt-budget limits even at 1800s IngestSummarySeconds (253KB prompt, ~63K tokens on a 32K context model). The underlying issue is prompt size, not latency: the model literally cannot see the whole package. Fix: in the ingest module chunker, compute an estimated prompt byte budget as NumCtx*0.6*4 (≈78KB at num_ctx=32768) and, when a per-package module exceeds it, fall back to per-file summaries for that package only. Keeps the existing per-package strategy for small packages where holistic summaries are more useful. No AST needed — pure size gate. Acceptance: cortex self-ingest at cmd/cortex commit 4d34969 completes with 0 SUMMARIZER_FAILED errors on a local qwen3:4b at num_ctx=32768. File under internal/ingest, touches pipeline.go and/or the module planner.

title: recall: composite activation has no 'no relevance' floor — negative queries never empty
description: Deep eval baseline (cortex-0jm, eval/deep/runs/baseline.json) shows negative queries — graphql subscriptions, oauth2, react components, kafka partitions, enterprise billing — all returned eval-artifact hits at composite score ~0.30-0.37. None of these concepts exist in cortex. Negative-trigger rate: 0/5. Root cause hypothesis: composite = 0.3*base + 0.3*ppr + 0.3*sim + 0.1*importance has no minimum-similarity gate, so a query with zero real matches still ranks by the floor activation of whatever PPR touches. Investigation: (1) measure similarity score distribution for negative vs positive queries; (2) consider a minimum-similarity precondition before the PPR+ACT-R rerank; (3) or clamp the composite when max(similarity, ppr) is below a threshold. Re-verify with deep eval after fix — target negative_triggered >= 0.8 (4/5).

title: ingest: exclude eval/ artifacts via .cortexignore (eval self-recall)
description: Deep eval baseline (cortex-0jm, eval/deep/runs/baseline.json) shows eval/results.baseline.json, eval/results.json, eval/questions.json, and eval/run_eval.sh dominate rank-1 for 21 of 30 questions. These files carry verbatim keyword lists and act as lexical magnets because they were ingested as per-file modules. Fix: add eval/results*.json, eval/questions*.json, eval/run_eval.sh, and eval/deep/runs/*.json to .cortexignore; rebuild index; re-run bash eval/deep/run_deep_eval.sh and confirm P@1 improves. This is the single biggest contributor to the current 0.16 P@1 and 0/5 negative-trigger rate.

title: ingest pipeline missing Neo4j wiring → no MENTIONS edges, recall returns empty
description:
cmd/cortex/ingest.go:254 buildIngestPipeline constructs a write.Pipeline with ConceptsEnabled:true but does NOT set the Neo4j field. The condition in internal/write/pipeline.go is 'p.ConceptsEnabled && p.Neo4j != nil', so deriveAndAppendConcepts never runs for any ingested entry. Result: ingested module entries get NO mentions_concept datoms in the log, NO Concept→Entry MENTIONS edges in Neo4j, seed resolution returns [] for all queries, PPR runs against empty seeds, and cortex recall returns 0 results for everything.

Reproduced via 100-question eval against freshly-ingested cortex repo: 100/100 EMPTY, 0% pass rate. Direct Neo4j query confirms 150 of 156 facet_project=cortex entries have zero outgoing MENTIONS relationships.

The Weaviate, Neighbors, LinkProposer, and ExpectedEmbeddingDim fields are also missing from the ingest write.Pipeline (compare buildObservePipeline at cmd/cortex/observe.go:198-216), which is why only 3/150 ingest entries have vectors in Weaviate.
design: Mirror the field set from cmd/cortex/observe.go:198-216 in cmd/cortex/ingest.go:254. Specifically wire: Neo4j (neo4j.NewBackendApplier(bolt)), Weaviate (weaviate.NewBackendApplier(weaviateClient)), Neighbors, LinkProposer, LinkConfig, LinkTopK, ExpectedEmbeddingDim. Ingest must use the SAME write pipeline shape as observe so concept extraction and vector storage happen identically.
acceptance_criteria: buildIngestPipeline wires Neo4j (and Weaviate + ExpectedEmbeddingDim) into the write.Pipeline; re-ingest produces mentions_concept datoms in the log; Neo4j shows MENTIONS edges from ingest entries; cortex recall returns non-empty results for queries that match ingested module bodies
close_reason: Wired Neo4j (BackendApplier), Weaviate (BackendApplier), Neighbors, LinkProposer, LinkConfig, LinkTopK, ExpectedEmbeddingDim into buildIngestPipeline's write.Pipeline at cmd/cortex/ingest.go:254. Mirrors buildObservePipeline. Verified: re-ingest produced 168 entries with 7308 MENTIONS edges (was 0). Recall now returns relevant results — eval went from 0/100 EMPTY to 9/100 PASS + 66/100 with semantic hits.

title: live bench recall p95=11s exceeds 2s envelope (per-call GDS projection refresh)
description:
cortex bench --live --corpus=small fails the recall stage:

  observe  PASS  p95=878ms  envelope=3s  n=200
  recall   FAIL  p95=11.007s envelope=2s  n=200

Root cause: neo4jPPRRunner.refreshProjection drops and re-projects the GDS graph on every single Run() call. Under the bench load (200 recalls), the cumulative projection cost blows the envelope. The comment in cmd/cortex/recall_adapters.go acknowledges this: 'a follow-up bead should move the refresh to a write-path hook so recall doesn't pay the cost on every call.'

Fix options: (a) invalidate projection only when write path mutates the semantic graph; (b) cache a generation counter on CortexMeta; (c) skip refresh when projection is up to date.
close_reason: Closed

title: cortex recall fails with PPR_FAILED even after observe + rebuild
description:
Running `cortex recall <query>` against a live stack (cortex up healthy, status green, 5 entries written via cortex observe) fails with:

  cortex: PPR_FAILED: personalized pagerank failed

This happens even after cortex-aqz (GDS projection bootstrap) merged. Re-running `cortex rebuild --accept-drift` (which scanned 98 datoms / 7 entries cleanly) does not fix the symptom — the next recall still fails with PPR_FAILED.

Reproduction (verified 2026-04-10 on main @ be4bd8b):
  cortex up                                 # healthy
  cortex observe "Retries must use exponential backoff with full jitter" --kind=Observation --facets=domain:Reliability,project:pay-gw
  cortex observe "Thundering herd at 09:42 on pay-gw service" --kind=ObservedRace --facets=domain:Reliability,project:pay-gw
  cortex recall "retry backoff"            # PPR_FAILED
  cortex rebuild --accept-drift             # ok, 7 entries
  cortex recall "retry backoff"            # PPR_FAILED again

Note: cortex bench --live with profile P1-dev passed all 200 recall ops in 2m3s — suggesting bench and standalone recall resolve the PPR projection differently (bench uses a pinned corpus; CLI recall hits the live graph).

Hypothesis: the GDS projection either (a) is not being refreshed after write-time :Concept/:MENTIONS edges are added, (b) does not cover the labels the recall PPR adapter queries, or (c) exists but returns zero when the seed set is empty, and the error classifier maps that to PPR_FAILED.

Why P1: cortex recall is the primary read path. A stack where observe succeeds but recall always errors is functionally unusable end-to-end.
notes: Caller: cmd/cortex/recall.go -> internal/recall/pipeline.Recall -> pipeline.PPR.Run. Investigate neo4jPPRRunner.Run in cmd/cortex/recall_adapters.go and the semanticGraphName projection bootstrap introduced by cortex-aqz.
close_reason: Fixed: refreshProjection now drops+rebuilds GDS projection on every Run, solving stale-snapshot failures when entries observed after first recall.

title: Expose Ollama num_ctx via config and wire it into Generate requests
description:
Ollama's default num_ctx is 2048 tokens. internal/ollama/client.go never sets num_ctx on Generate requests, so ALL generation workloads in cortex (link derivation, concept extraction, trail summary, ingest synthesis, analyze) are silently capped at a 2K effective context window regardless of the model's advertised context length (128K for llama3.1:8b, 256K for qwen3:4b-instruct-2507, etc.).

Impact:
- Trail summaries on long trails are truncated. buildTrailPromptBody concatenates every entry in a trail; anything past ~2K tokens is dropped before the model ever sees it, producing silent data loss in the summary.
- Link derivation on long observation bodies may truncate the source before it reaches the model, degrading link quality.
- Analyze command reasoning is similarly capped.
- The problem is invisible: Ollama does not error on over-long prompts, it just truncates from the head.

Fix scope:
1. internal/ollama/client.go Generate (and Embed if relevant): include "options": {"num_ctx": N} in the POST body to /api/generate.
2. internal/ollama/client.go Config struct: add NumCtx int (0 = Ollama default, explicit value = override).
3. internal/config/defaults.go Ollama section (or new OllamaConfig): add num_ctx YAML field with a sensible default (suggested: 8192 — enough for typical link derivation + concept extraction while keeping KV-cache RAM bounded to ~1 GB on an 8B q4 model).
4. All cmd/cortex/*.go call sites that build ollama.Config (observe.go, trail.go, ingest.go, recall_adapters.go, analyze_adapters.go, doctor.go, up.go, rebuild.go, status.go) must thread cfg.Ollama.NumCtx through.
5. Unit test: verify Generate request body contains options.num_ctx when configured.
6. Config loader test: default num_ctx present; override honored.

Why: spec integration gap discovered during cortex-0ci / pre-e2e review. Without this fix, the model selection discussion (llama3.1:8b 128K vs qwen3:4b-instruct-2507 256K) is theoretical — every model effectively runs with 2K context until num_ctx is wired through.
How to apply: add options block in doGenerateRequest, add config field, thread through call sites, add test asserting the request body shape.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ollama.Config has NumCtx field. 2. Generate POST body to /api/generate contains options.num_ctx when NumCtx > 0. 3. config YAML accepts ollama.num_ctx with a default. 4. All 9 call sites in cmd/cortex construct ollama.Config with NumCtx threaded from cfg. 5. Unit test asserts request body shape. 6. Config loader test asserts default and override.
notes: Related: cortex-0ci (CORTEX_TRAIL_ID env var), phantom llama3.1:8b-instruct tag fix. Discovered while evaluating qwen3:4b-instruct-2507 (256K context) — finding that 256K is irrelevant without num_ctx wired up. Default recommendation 8192 balances typical workload size against KV cache RAM (roughly 100-400 MB for 8K ctx on 4-8B q4 models).
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement SIGINT/SIGTERM graceful shutdown for long commands
description:
reflect, analyze, and ingest install signal handlers that complete or discard the current transaction group on SIGINT or SIGTERM and leave watermarks consistent before exit.

- Sending SIGINT mid-reflect either commits the current transaction group fully or discards it entirely (never partial)

close_reason: Implemented internal/shutdown package providing signal.NotifyContext-based graceful shutdown primitive. First signal cancels a caller-scoped context and logs SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED via an injected Logger interface; a second signal inside the 5s grace window routes to a HardExit callback (os.Exit(1) in production). Tests cover all acceptance criteria via an injected signal channel seam — real OS signal tests are deferred to the integration suite because Go's runtime uses signals for goroutine preemption. title: Implement cortex pin, unpin, evict, unevict commands

title: Implement recall --mode similar, traverse, path, community, surprise

cortex recall honors --mode=similar, traverse, path, community, and surprise, each invoking a distinct pipeline as specified in the BDD scenarios without running default-mode PPR reranking.

title: Implement activation decay, reinforcement, and visibility threshold
description:
An activation package seeds base_activation=1.0 on new entries, decays using d=0.5, reinforces base_activation, retrieval_count, and last_retrieved_at on recall, and excludes entries with base_activation < 0.05 from default retrieval while keeping them queryable via history, as-of, and --mode=surprise.

- **initial_encoding_time** (`time`): recorded at write -- Source: observe pipeline
- **retrieval_event_times** (`list<time>`): append per recall -- Source: recall pipeline

## Outputs

## Constraints
- Activation attributes use LWW replay semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- A freshly written entry has base_activation == 1.0 and retrieval_count == 0
- After 1 million seconds with zero retrievals, base_activation is below 0.05 and the entry is absent from default recall
- A retrieval event on a below-threshold entry can raise base_activation above 0.05 unless the entry is evicted
- Pinned entries do not decay below their pin-time activation

title: Implement A-MEM derived link scoring and persistence

The write pipeline queries the top 5 nearest neighbors from Weaviate, asks the LLM to score link candidates, persists only links with confidence >= 0.60 (and cosine >= 0.75 for SIMILAR_TO), and skips derived-link writes when the LLM returns malformed output.

- Respects timeouts.link_derivation_seconds

title: Implement cortex rebuild with pinned-model enforcement
description:
cortex rebuild replays the datom log into fresh derived Neo4j and Weaviate state using the embedding_model_digest recorded on each entry, fails with pinned-model-drift when the current installed digest differs, and with --accept-drift re-embeds affected entries while writing model_rebind audit datoms.

## Inputs
- **log_reader** (`iterator<Datom>`): full merge-sorted stream -- Source: log-merge-sort-reader
- **accept_drift** (`bool`): default false -- Source: --accept-drift flag

## Outputs
- **fresh_backends** (`Backends`): Neo4j and Weaviate staging then active -- Dest: backends
- **model_rebind_datoms** (`list<Datom>`): only when --accept-drift -- Dest: log

## Constraints
- Staging namespace used until rebuild completes; swap is atomic
acceptance_criteria:
- rebuild on a log recorded under digest sha256:aaa fails with PINNED_MODEL_DRIFT when the current Ollama digest is sha256:bbb and --accept-drift is absent
- rebuild --accept-drift on that same log re-embeds affected entries and writes one model_rebind datom per re-embedded entry
- rebuild into a staging namespace leaves the active derived index untouched until the rebuild succeeds
- rebuild is idempotent (running twice in a row produces byte-identical Layer 1 output)
close_reason: rebuild package with PINNED_MODEL_DRIFT enforcement, --accept-drift re-embed audit datoms, staging-vs-active isolation, idempotency. CLI shell wired (live staging adapter is a follow-up). 8 package tests passing.

title: Implement cortex merge command for external log segments

title: Implement segment quarantine and tx ULID collision detection

title: Implement built-in language grouping strategies

An ingest strategies package groups walked files into modules per built-in language strategy (Go per-package, Java per-class, Python per-file, etc.) selected by file extension with a per-file fallback.
